data.body:
PASS 1 review (E4 — assumption/premise verification) of the "Document reusable machinery API reference" doc plan.

METHOD: every codebase assertion in the plan was probed with Read/Grep against the named implementation files (no reliance on training knowledge). The technical descriptions are accurate, but three load-bearing premises about the repo's CURRENT state are contradicted, and one named parameter does not exist.

FINDINGS:

- [ ] The plan's central premise — Why: "there is no single developer-facing API REFERENCE describing the reuse surface itself … This ticket creates that reference" + AC-1 "A developer-facing API reference exists (new doc under docs/, e.g. docs/reuse-surface.md …)" — is FALSE against current source: docs/reuse-surface.md ALREADY EXISTS and already covers all four subsystems for both human + LLM audiences. Re-scope from CREATE to AUDIT/VERIFY/EXTEND the existing docs/reuse-surface.md, and reword AC-1 from "exists (new doc)" to "is verified/extended".
  - criteria: [E4]
  - location: plan "Why" section + Acceptance Criteria line 1; evidence file docs/reuse-surface.md
  - evidence: ["docs/reuse-surface.md:1 titled '# Reusable machinery — developer API reference'", "docs/reuse-surface.md:3-6 covers 'the HMAC signing surface, the LLM workflow runtime (the runner + the declarative workflow executor), the prompt/contract model, and the output-schema seam' — the same four sections this plan enumerates", "docs/reuse-surface.md:16 'Audience: human developers and LLM agents'"]

- [ ] AC-6's cross-link clauses are the GENUINELY-UNMET gap — re-target the ticket here rather than at fresh authorship. The existing docs/reuse-surface.md is NOT cross-linked from docs/llm-framework.md and NOT listed in CLAUDE.md (the existing doc links OUT to llm-framework.md, but neither file links back).
  - criteria: [E4]
  - location: Acceptance Criteria line 6 ("Cross-linked from docs/llm-framework.md and listed in CLAUDE.md's docs pointers")
  - evidence: ["grep 'reuse-surface' in docs/llm-framework.md → no matches", "grep 'reuse-surface' in CLAUDE.md → no matches"]

- [ ] AC-7's automated drift-check test does NOT exist — confirm before claiming AC-7 satisfied. The existing doc ASSERTS this test, so the claim is currently stale.
  - criteria: [E4]
  - location: Acceptance Criteria line 7 ("verified … by an AUTOMATED drift-check test (tests/unit/test_reuse_surface_doc.py, run in CI)")
  - evidence: ["grep 'reuse_surface' under tests/ → no matches", "docs/reuse-surface.md:17-18 asserts the CI gate 'tests/unit/test_reuse_surface_doc.py' that introspects each documented callable, but the named test file is absent — so AC-7 is the real remaining work, not a met claim"]

- [ ] Scope item 2 names a run_workflow parameter that does not exist — "run_workflow (the declarative-IR executor: inputs/run_id/dry_run, RunResult, step kinds)". There is no dry_run argument on run_workflow; dry-run, if it exists, is a workflow-doc/step concept, not a run_workflow arg. Drop dry_run from the scope description or name the real dry-run mechanism so the doc does not assert a non-existent parameter.
  - criteria: [E4]
  - location: plan "What / Scope" item 2
  - evidence: ["src/rebar/llm/workflow/executor.py:584 run_workflow(doc, inputs, *, run_id=None, target_ticket=None, repo_root=None, scripted_registry=None, agent_runner=None, recorder=None, secrets=None) — no dry_run parameter"]

AFFIRMATIONS (E4 — assertions VERIFIED TRUE against source):
- Signing surface (src/rebar/signing.py): all 8 named functions exist with the asserted shapes — sign_manifest (:319), verify_signature (:362), verify_record (:208), signing_key (:79), key_fingerprint (:151), compute_signature (:202), parse_manifest (:161), head_sha (:298). Manifest format (JSON array of non-empty strings), HMAC-SHA256 + PAYLOAD_VERSION=1 sorted-key compact-JSON canonicalisation, REBAR_SIGNING_KEY/.signing-key custody, certified|mismatch|foreign_key|unsigned verdict vocab, SIGNATURE event + last-writer-wins replay, head_sha audit/staleness binding — all match source.
- LLM runtime (src/rebar/llm/runner.py): Runner protocol (run/preflight, :71), RunRequest with every named field mode/execution_mode/output_schema/extra_tools (:28), get_runner (:236), PydanticAIRunner (:132), structured.py present. run_workflow + RunResult (workflow/executor.py:584). verify_completion single-call op (src/rebar/llm/completion.py:169).
- Prompt/contract model (src/rebar/llm/prompts.py): get_prompt (:173), resolve_prompt (:648), closed FRONT_MATTER_KEYS contract incl. inputs/outputs/execution_mode/category, EXECUTION_MODES enum (:51), .rebar/prompts/<id>.md project>built-in override resolution, derived index + regenerate_prompt_index + `python -m rebar.llm.prompts` CI drift gate.
- Output-schema seam: validate_structured (src/rebar/llm/findings.py:247), register_contract/response_model_for (src/rebar/llm/contracts.py:27/33), packaged JSON Schemas at src/rebar/schemas/*.schema.json, resolve_citations + finding normalization (findings.py:146).

Net: the API descriptions are sound and the symbols are real; the staleness is in the plan's premise that the deliverable is NEW and in two unmet ACs (cross-links + drift test) plus the non-existent dry_run param. Re-scope to the verified gaps.
